---
title: "Getting port 80 to forward to 8080 with iptables"
date: 2008-10-30
forum: Server Platforms
---

### Post by needhelpplease on 2008-10-30
My web servers are all in Java so I'm trying to get port 80 to forward to port 8080, so I won't have to run them as root.  This is all under Ubuntu Server 8.04.

I used the trick about redirect.  Here's what my tables look like:

```
# iptables-save
# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.8 on Thu Oct 30 18:49:46 2008
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [1791:129072]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [982:72587]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [976:72227]
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j REDIRECT --to-ports 8080
COMMIT
# Completed on Thu Oct 30 18:49:46 2008
# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.8 on Thu Oct 30 18:49:46 2008
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [972:132614]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [7419:2597562]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 143 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 25 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 10025 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j DROP
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
# Completed on Thu Oct 30 18:49:46 2008
```

But there is no luck, it does not forward.  Any suggestions?

---

### Post by needhelpplease on 2008-10-30
Ah, a stupid and obvious mistake.  After the redirect happens, it's as if the packet came in on port 8080, so there must be an allow rule for that *before* the drop-everything rule.

---
